title: The Marketing Meeting
summary: The marketing meeting covered website progress, messaging, and product differentiation, with a focus on conveying a "human first or AI first" message for the Straw solution. Vinnie Micciche expressed frustration with the website's progress and directed Michael Donovan to implement the provided content immediately, while Michael Donovan explained the foundational work completed and technical issues with the existing website, leading to tension. The team agreed to reconnect tomorrow to decompress and clarify the scope of work.

decisions.description: Implement website content with a "human first or AI first" message.
Build the website with the content provided by Vinnie Micciche.
Reconnect tomorrow to discuss the scope of work.
Focus on refreshing the existing website for a quick win.

actionItems.task: Throw a canvas together for Slack to drop ideas for visual inspiration for the website.

expectedOutcome.description: A dedicated Slack channel or canvas filled with visual inspiration for the website design, covering BDC, service, and AI aspects.

actionItems.task: Work on the social posts and email blast to generate business.

expectedOutcome.description: Scheduled social media posts and email campaigns designed to attract new business and generate leads.

actionItems.task: Build the website exactly with the content that Vinnie Micciche shared.

expectedOutcome.description: The website is updated with the exact content provided by Vinnie Micciche, reflecting the intended messaging and product differentiation.

actionItems.task: Put something together if there is a scope of work that Joe Furnari would like.

expectedOutcome.description: A document outlining the proposed scope of work, including specific tasks, deadlines, and responsibilities.

tensions: Vinnie Micciche's frustration over lack of visible website progress.
Michael Donovan's feeling of being targeted and overwhelmed by low-level tasks.
Disagreement over the best approach to website improvement (refresh vs. redesign).
Conflicting priorities between short-term content updates and long-term performance improvements.
Michael Donovan's concerns about resource constraints and their role as VP of Marketing versus individual contributor.
